type: SURVEY
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Adding new module-level 'ssn' Regex type definition after the existing isbn type (around line 70). Purely additive - a new standalone variable assignment, not modifying isbn itself.

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Adding new hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') definition, a purely additive line/block appended near the end of the file (after isbn). No changes to existing lines expected.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: end-of-file additions after isbn
code: 
# Matches a US Social Security Number in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: exactly 64 hexadecimal characters
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

note: Proposed order: ssn first (matches your survey position right after isbn), then hash_sha256 appended after it. Let me know if you want a different order/comment style for ssn since I do not know your exact pattern.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: insertion block after isbn definition
code: isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash represented as 64 hexadecimal characters.
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")
note: Proposed order: isbn (unchanged, shown for context) then ssn then hash_sha256, each with a one-line comment. Please confirm identical text or propose changes.
